title: Generics, Type Sets, Constraints, Inference, and Iteration · Language Lens: Generic Operations Limits
summary: A collection library needs reusable operations without reflection, lost relationships, over-broad constraints, or unreadable APIs. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go generic operations limits increment in a constrained reusable collection library. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use only operations guaranteed by the type set and explain why fields, methods, conversions, and nil differ across type arguments.
Build minimal constraints from method elements, unions, underlying-type approximations, comparable, and intersection semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
